Review Content: I chose this cleaner at the advice of our orthodontist for cleaning my child’s Invisalign trays and retainers. Compared with other options, I’d buy this again since it works well and is very affordable.

Review Content: I purchased the Exluvon ultrasonic retainer cleaner for my daughter. We have been using it for over a month now and are very satisfied with it. I have used it with just plain water on some jewelry and it did make a difference on a gold and diamond ring and on silver tarnish on a necklace, but it would probably be most effective to ad jewelry cleaner for heavy tarnish. Overall the ultrasonic cleaner is a great value for the price compared to other brands . It feels like a quality made product.

Review Content: This is a must have for anyone with retainers!! The best part is you can use it to clean just about anythjng not only retainers. Overall it's a great buy, simple, efficient, and quick, five stars!!!

Name: Angels Are Real
Review Content: My 3 teenaged children and I all wear retainers at night. This little pod cleaned our retainers so well. Mine was cleaner looking than when I received it from the company brand new! We only get one set of teeth and putting a dirty retainer in your mouth for 8+ hours will wreak havoc on your mouth. This little pod is easy and quiet (I’d say it’s a quick ticking sound if I had to define the sound it makes.). And it’s a good price - it seems like it will last 5+ years easily. And only takes 5 minutes to clean the retainers.

Name: Honest reviews
Review Content: Click to play video The 'description' on this item gave no clue as to what it was, the picture wasn't much help. I hoped it might be an ultrasonic cleaner and it is - happy days! I had small items that needed cleaning and they would have fallen through the gaps in the basket. I found some plastic mesh that I had made fly screens from and lined the basket with that and it worked perfectly. I put my earrings through 2 x 5 minutes cycles (almost exactly 5 minutes each when I ran it). I only cleaned one of the pair to be able to see if there was a difference .... and there definitely is! This was with water only. I ran all of my jewellery thorough another few cycles and everything is wonderfully clean. Read more

Review Content: Easy to use and effective too. I use this for my mouth guard, my whitening shields from the dentist and for my jewellery. Brings items up gleaming in a short time. Thank you!

Name: Ernestina
Review Content: I was very much in hope that this was an ultrasonic cleaner when it was offered to review. It wasnt at all certain as there is almost no information , not even the size , only the name . I have been wanting to try one for cleaning the coffee filter pucks that fit in our expresso machine as they seem very hard to clean due to their fineness. You can clean using water or Ultrasonic cleaning liquids. There are some online videos of people making their own liquids so I went with that. The cleaner isnt huge, it is around 5" square outside dimensions and the bath section is round. The bath section is 4cm deep and 8.5cm diameter. Anyway when it came it has a little round basket with a handle in the middle making it too small for the coffee filters but if I dont use the basket it is a perfect machine for what I wanted it for. The paperwork that comes with it suggests you use it for one use then wait 5 mins before the next one. It is timed for a 5 min clean each time you press on. This actually does a good job of clearing the weeny little holes in the filters and so I shall be keeping it exclusively for that. These can be used for cleaning dental inserts, baby dummys, jewellery , gum shields, ear and nose clips, small toys, nuts and small bolts. You get a 12v 2A 3 pin transformer plug with it and a pair of plastic tweesers. The cage basket insert fits the bath area perfectly but isnt really needed, it just helps keep things from rubbing against the bottom or each other. Cant comment on value for money as no price available at present Read more

I’m already using cleaning tablets, which work just fine in a bowl of water. I bought this so I wouldn’t have to keep buying new tablets. Also, on my second use of the device, it began alternating between a gentle purr, which you would expect, and a grinding noise, which made me think the motor was already having trouble.

Review Content: This was excellent for cleaning my trays during Invisalign treatment and helped me feel fresh!

Name: Big bear M
Review Content: I use retainers daily, and cleaning them properly with just a toothbrush never really feels enough, especially around small crevices and buildup areas. After using this ultrasonic retainer cleaner for a while, I can definitely tell the difference in how much fresher and cleaner my retainers feel. The 49KHz ultrasonic cleaning works very well for removing residue that regular brushing misses. I especially noticed improvement around tighter corners and hard-to-clean spots. When paired with cleaning tablets, it works even better for deeper cleaning and odor removal. I also like the addition of the built-in UV lamps. The combination of ultrasonic cleaning and UV treatment helps the retainers feel fresher after each cycle, especially after overnight wear. One feature I really appreciate is the small viewing window on the lid. It allows you to see inside while the machine is running, which makes it easier to monitor the cleaning process instead of completely guessing what’s happening inside. The multiple cleaning modes are practical too. I usually use the quick clean mode for daily maintenance and the deeper cleaning mode occasionally when I want a more thorough cleaning session. Another thing I appreciate is how quiet the machine is during operation. It runs much more quietly than I expected and doesn’t create much disturbance on the bathroom counter or desk. Besides retainers, I’ve also been using it to clean electric toothbrush heads, which works surprisingly well for removing buildup around the bristles and keeping them fresher between replacements. The dual-slot lid design is thoughtful because it allows storage or cleaning for toothbrushes alongside dental appliances. The included tweezers are also useful for more hygienic handling. The 220ML stainless steel tank feels durable and is large enough for retainers, mouth guards, aligners, and even small jewelry items. The compact size also makes it easy to store or pack for travel. Overall, this is a very convenient and effective ultrasonic cleaner that makes retainer, toothbrush head, and dental appliance maintenance much easier and more hygienic. Read more

Name: Loous
Review Content: I bought this ultrasonic cleaner mainly for my night guard and retainer because brushing alone did not always make them feel completely clean. The machine is small enough to keep on the bathroom counter, and the 220ML size works well for dental items without taking up much space. I add water, place the retainer inside, set the timer, and let it run while I get ready in the morning. The cleaning process is simple, and I like that I do not have to scrub as much by hand. After using it, my retainer feels fresher and cleaner, especially around small grooves that are harder to reach with a toothbrush. I also tried it with a few pieces of jewelry, and it helped remove light buildup. The digital timer is easy to read, and the noise level is acceptable for bathroom use. For daily retainer, night guard, and small accessory cleaning, it has been convenient and easy to use.

Review Content: Probably one of my favorite and most used products I’ve ever purchased. I know this is a late review, but I’ve had it for several years now and still use it regularly for my Invisalign retainers. It works exactly as expected and has held up really well over time. The first time I used it, I was honestly shocked at how much buildup it removed. I thought my retainers were already clean, but this showed me otherwise. Since then, it’s become part of my routine and keeps everything feeling fresh. Super easy to use, reliable, and definitely worth it if you wear retainers or any similar dental appliances.

Name: Meagan A
Review Content: I am giving this 5 stars and will update if needed in the future. 6/23 I REALLY appreciate legit reviews with pictures and videos so I am making an effort to review more to help others. Lots of folks post about these ultrasonic machines using relatively new Invisalign-type retainers which just isn’t going to compare to a night guard or multiple-year-old retainer. I read hundreds of reviews before deciding on this one and forgoing a used professional dentist-grade machine on eBay. The negative first: I don’t LOVE that I can’t remove the tank to drain and rinse but I won’t ding them for that unless it becomes an issue later. Positives: relatively small realty space, quiet, easy to use, seems to do the job. My guard is almost 3 years old and has darkening stains in the molars especially. I typically brush it with a toothbrush, a scant amount of toothpaste and cool water. I used all the retainer powder provided and did 4 cycles. The first two cycles I used the retainer powder provided. I gave it a 10-15 min break her the instructions to cool down then ran it two more times using half of a retainer brite tablet each time. I’m super impressed. Hard to tell via images but it did VERY well removing the darkened spots almost completely. I’m sure repeated use will get the job done entirely.

Name: Jesus Navarro
Review Content: I use these for my retainers. They are the clear Invisalign-type retainers. You fill with water, toss your retainers or whatever in there and turn it on. It turns off automatically after 5 minutes. Then remove your retainers, rinse them off and that's it. I do this every night and my retainers stay nice and clean. Cleaning the device itself is annoying. Apparently you used to be able to remove the water reservoir but not anymore. That means you need to unplug and grab the entire thing to pour out the left over water. Cleaning is a bit annoying as well because you want to make sure you don't spill too much water over the sides and cause any water damager. But otherwise it's not too bad. Once a week I throw in my retainers as well as a denture tablet. Mine came with a few tablets but I also grabbed a pack of walmart tablets and they work just fine. Then I fill with water, toss a tablet, then put the retainers in. Put them in the machine for three cycles: two back-to-back 5-minute cycles, then wait 5 minutes for the machine to cool down, then one final 5-minute cycle. Then remove the retainers and rinse + clean out the denture water from the machine. It's a shame you can't remove the water reservoir anymore because that would make cleaning much easier, but from what I understand they did this because too many people were messing up their devices. Oh well. Still a good purchase. Read more

Name: tlb
Review Content: I’ve been very happy with this. I’ve used it daily for about 6 months. It definitely seems “stronger” (finger tingles) and to be more effective than another ultrasonic cleaner I bought a few months before this one. Once I tried this one, I gave away the older one. My aligner trays stay much more clear now. I do use this in conjunction with a 1/2 of an iSonic cleaning tablet. Its stainless steel lining is easy to clean. Name: Amazon Reviewer
Review Content: Historically I have cleaned my retainer by soaking it in vinegar when I think of it. My dentist was surprised by how clean it was considering it was 15 years old. I've seen ads for these from all kinds of brands, and decided at $19.99 it was worth a shot. Plaque build up is a thing. First impression was that the instructions and the machine don't quite line up. It says to fill between the min and max line, but there is only a max line. It sounds like those jewelery cleaners that you can hear constantly in the back of shops, and has a UV setting to theoretically kill bacteria. As someone who is autistic and overstimulated by noise very easily, the sound of this is grating. I hate it passionately, but am dealing with it/making accomodations while it runs in hopes for cleanliness. Fun fact: these cleaners are the number one reason I don't go into jeweler shops. Not only do I not need to buy jewelery, but even looking drives me nuts because of these. Back to to important stuff: I don't have an incubator and petri dish to test if the UV on this machine actually does a good job killing all those nasties. It does fit both my retainers at the same time - I have a removable top and bottom. I can't tell visually that it is any cleaner than it was. The plaque is still present even after 15 minutes of run time (initial 5, followed by another 10 in case maybe because of age this would help loosen things up). It does mention in the instructions that jewelry should be run on the 10 min setting. This tells me it does jewelry, I wasn't personally planning on using this for that, but sure! My wedding ring hasn't been cleaned since I bought it. I can't tell a difference in its cleaning either. It looks just as it was when I put it in. For both cleanings the water got increasingly warm, something is happening, but I'm not really sure its cleaning anything better than some vinegar would. UPDATE: I still cant confirm if the UV is real, but over time it has completely removed the visible plaque from my retainers. I havent repeated a cleaning of jewelery, but it is clearly doing something to get the retainer build up off. Read more

Review Content: Ultra compact, je m'attendais à plus grand mais c'est ma faute car je n'ai pas lu la description comme il faut, patins anti-dérapants. Name: Phili
Review Content: Ich sag’s dir ehrlich – ich hab schon etliche Reinigungsgeräte für Zahnschienen ausprobiert, und die meisten sind laut, langsam oder bringen einfach kein richtig sauberes Ergebnis. Das Beser Ultraschallreinigungsgerät dagegen ist richtig stark. Klein, modern, effizient – genau so stell ich mir ein Alltags-Gadget vor, das man wirklich nutzt. Die Reinigungsleistung ist beeindruckend. Innerhalb weniger Minuten sind Zahnschienen, Retainer oder Aufbissschienen wirklich sauber – kein Schleier, keine Rückstände. Die Kombination aus 2x Ultraschallreinigung und 4x Sterilisation macht hier den Unterschied. Du siehst, dass Bakterien und Beläge wirklich verschwinden, und das Teil arbeitet dabei leise und kontrolliert. Ich feiere das Design – kompakt, hochwertig und superleicht zu bedienen. Ein Knopf, klare LED-Anzeige, fertig. Kein kompliziertes Menü, kein App-Zwang, einfach Technik, die funktioniert. Und das Gehäuse sieht so clean aus, dass du’s problemlos im Bad stehen lassen kannst. Die Sterilisationsfunktion ist das, was andere Geräte oft versprechen, aber selten liefern. Hier merkst du den Unterschied: kein Geruch, keine Ablagerungen, einfach hygienisch rein. Gerade wenn du Zahnschienen täglich nutzt, ist das ein echter Gamechanger. Auch stark: der Energieverbrauch. Das Gerät läuft effizient, braucht kaum Strom und überhitzt nicht – perfekt für den täglichen Einsatz. Unterm Strich: Das Beser Ultraschallreinigungsgerät ist für mich kein typisches „Nice-to-have“, sondern ein echtes Upgrade in Sachen Hygiene und Komfort. Schnell, leise, effektiv – ganz klare 5 Sterne, weil es einfach professionell reinigt und zuverlässig sterilisiert. Read more

Review Content: I have a rather wide mouth and this fits my retainer fairly easily. Easy to setup and use. Noise a little loud but, acceptable. It does the job needed to clean as I want.

Name: DAVID A MAIR
Review Content: For cleaning dental appliances professional advice suggests using something like this only two or three times a week using just water but once a week add a soluble cleaning tablet to the water. The manufacturer recommends only using their own cleaning tablets that dissolve in water but I expect most commercial soluble dental appliance cleaning tablets will be fine. This just adds a bit of very high-speed "shake" while the dental appliance is in the water or water with cleaning tablet. The manual says it is fine to use tap water, but my home tap water is softened, low calcium but high sodium which probably explains why my night guard appeared to be turning yellow. When I got this was the first time I thought to use water from my reverse osmosis (RO) water filter with the filter tablets and my night guard seems to be looking better. The RO filtered water has less than 10% of the dissolved solids as my softened home water from normal faucets. I haven't used it long enough to say much about reliability. The manual says not to drop it but the lid opens with very little force and rests almost vertical so it is very easy to accidentally nudge the lid and requires very little force to cause it to effectively "drop" on the base. It would have added little cost to have a simple detent or tension to the lid hinge to let it stay up when touched accidentally. I suspect the UVC light will fail before the ultrasonic vibration. The manual also makes very clear not to get liquid from the cleaning container on the outer plastic case, but the cleaning container is circular so unless you turn it upside down extremely quickly from a high enough height to prevent splashes reaching it then it's almost impossible to prevent getting water on the outside cover and even bridging the stainless steel cleaning container to the plastic case when draining it. It would also have been very little additional cost to have a cleaning container shape that creates a spout opposite the hinge to allow draining it with minimal placing of the cleaning solution over the outside case. I bought a very cheap other model with a rectangular shape that allows the front corners to act as a simple spout when draining. The power supply runs warm after only two consecutive uses, so it's probably wise to have it totally disconnected (recommended by the manual when not in-use anyway) and store both the cleaner and PSU in a place safe from dropping when not using it and only take them out and connect them when needed. The sound level is much lower than I expected and it is quite comfortable to be standing net to it in a small room when it's running. A conversation could be held next to it while running without raising voices. I would have paid the extra that could have delivered a lid hinge detent/tension and spout for draining. Hence the 4 star but these are relatively minor distractions and it seems to do a decent job on my night guard. Read more

Review Content: It looks nice, but I'm convinced it does absolutely nothing. I picked this one for the fact it had the countdown visible, "uv", a couple inserts for toothbrush heads etc. But what I really needed it for was for retainers and invisalign trays. Sadly, even with retainer cleaner, I have to resort to brushing my liners and my kids retainers with toothpaste/toothbrush. Which can eventually lead to damage. Which is why I purchased this, to hopefully save money. I have very good oral hygiene health and brush/floss/2x per day floss after every meal. I have no issues with Tartar. I'm getting new invisalign trays every 2 weeks to fix some tiny gaps from not wearing retainers after braces as a kid , and I truly thought this equipment would be time saving. I don't eat with my trays in and do not smoke etc. I only drink coffee in am THEN brush my teeth then insert trays. Everything else is water. I've tried retainer tabs, liquid, just water in this machine, but nothing gets very minimal build up off of anything. The other thing I noticed is that I have used cold water and lukewarm water. Never hot. And this heats up the water which is a HUGE no because it will warp your appliances. Essentially it's garbage and do not waste your money. I purchased this in August- and the machine totally pooped out on me within 3 months of buying. Not shocking since I had been using it daily and it still wasn't working or making them fresh/clean. Name: JT
Review Content: I wish I had taken a picture. Honestly, I only started looking into these because my daughter asked for one, but I initially dismissed it because she is always asking for things and I figured I've been using tablets on my appliance for years and its fine so why would she need one?! Then I saw an ad and started looking into them. I broke down and it was amazing. Even with daily efferdent treatments, my appliance displayed a stunning improvement after one use of the Dental Pod PRO. I had no idea there was any build up at all (it wasn't yellow and didn't seem to be cloudy) but now it is so clear and clean it looks brand new. The removable reservoir to dump and refill is great. I love not having to worry about getting water on the cord/electrical components or keeping a long cord on the counter so I can reach the sink. The shorter daily clean is a great option, especially if you have to wear them during the day as it's quick enough to run while you brush your teeth, or shower, or do makeup, and then pop in the clean retainers if your appliance must be worn 24/7. I still use this function daily, even though I only need to wear mine at night, but it seems to be more than sufficient for regular cleaning. The first cleaning I used the proprietary tablets that came with it. However, I have since gone back to using efferdent. Both did great, and I may update and see if I prefer one or the other with a weekly cleaning as I've decided I'm comfortable with a water only daily clean.

Name: A.M.H.
Review Content: I have used 2 cheaper pods. They didn’t seem to work any better than simple soaking, and they broke quickly. This Zima pod is really so much better! My night guard is sparkling clean. The pod is easy to fill, empty, and clean. Operation is likewise simple and straightforward. And so far this pod is working perfectly. I feel that the Zima was a worthwhile investment.

Name: RR
Review Content: I am kicking myself for not buying this Zima Dental Pod Pro earlier. After the first couple of uses, I saw immediate results, and my dental devices are cleaner than they have ever been. It was quite an investment, so I am very glad the pod is performing so well and providing powerful, deep cleans of all my dental devices. Before this dental pod, I had been using the old-fashioned vinegar soaking method, but it never removed all of the built-up debris. I also used a toothbrush to scrub, without realizing that brushing can cause scratches on the retainers’ surfaces that can trap bacteria. I purchased this Zima Dental Pod Pro because I wanted deep cleaning without any damage. My new retainers are expensive, and my goal is to keep them in good shape as long as possible. The dental pod’s ultrasonic cleaning removes bacteria and built-up plaque without causing abrasion. After using it for a month and a half, I can confirm it leaves retainers and other dental devices much cleaner. There are two cleaning cycles with different speed times and auto-turn-offs. The first standard cycle has a duration of 5 minutes for daily cleaning. The second cycle is a 15-minute max clean mode that employs ultrasonic bursts for deep cleaning. I mostly use this second option for my older retainer, which had accumulated pretty heavy stains and buildup over the years. Zima dental sterilizing tablets can be added for a 10-minute pre-soak to increase the cleaning power, but they are not required for every cycle (*a small packet of tablets is included). The 15-minute max clean mode did not immediately eliminate all the stains on my very old retainer on the first try. However, after several sessions, I did notice that the stains became progressively lighter. I can also see that the old buildup and visible scaling has been removed. A great feature is the small, removable tank. It can be emptied, rinsed, and refilled with ease. The detachable tank allows you to place the pod outside the bathroom if you are short on space. The tank is compact but can hold my two biggest retainers. The pod is designed to thoroughly clean two dental appliances at one time, which is a huge plus. The pod itself is sleek, pleasing to look at, and feels durable. It is unobtrusive and does not take up much space. As for minor downsides, I would have liked easier-to-see min and max water fill marks. There are only two faint marks to indicate the optimal water levels (please see accompanying picture). In addition, a longer power cord would have made this pod easier to use in different areas. Also, during a cleaning cycle, the pod emits a buzzing, humming sound that is not very loud but could be an issue for people sensitive to sounds. Lastly, the sterilizing tablets, while not required for every cycle, are an ongoing expense. None of these issues were deal breakers for me since the pod does such a great job cleaning, but I thought it was important to note. In sum, this Zima dental pod pro is working perfectly and keeps my dental implements as clean as possible. A great investment if you want to deep clean your dental devices. Highly recommend. Read more

Name: Mr. Tudball
Review Content: The first time that I used this great product, I was amazed at the amount of gunk that came off my Invisalign retainers. I had just been soaking them in cleaning solution and periodically scrubbing them clean (or so I thought) with a dedicated toothbrush. Their company recommends that you run their cleaning cycle twice for a few days when you first start using the machine. My retainers looked brand new, were nice and clear again, and were odor free. Great product. Later, I also purchased this company's Ultraviolet Retainer Storage unit that cleans my retainers even more. Just a wonderful set of products that protect my teeth and dental health.

Name: Louis
Review Content: Great product. I use it every day. Fits both of my retainers. Design is pleasing and doesn’t look like an eye sore in my counter. I like the fact that the chamber disconnects from the base so you can easily fill it up and empty it without moving the entire device plugged in. Chamber is versatile enough to handle variety of size appliances. Build quality seems nice compared to others that I’ve had in past.

Name: Ken
Review Content: Does EVERYTHING I was annoyed about in the standard version. Hate unplugging your pod to clean/rinse? Now it’s two separate pieces. It’s bigger and has more space too. Better and quieter? Yup. With water running I don’t even hear it anymore. It still has the 5 minute interval but feels like it cleans faster and better. My ONE complaint, the on button is not visible from my sink as it’s slightly angled down. Just note it’s opposite of the cable, but I still miss the button every once in a while

Name: Amazon Customer
Review Content: It only comes with a 13am plug which means one cannot use it in the bathroom as one only have a razor socket surely this is a little silly in the uk Zima Dental pod pro Modular and Multi Mode

Name: Joanne Westover
Review Content: I’ve been using the ZIMA Dental Pod Pro for a week now, and overall I’m very impressed with its performance and design. The device is compact, modern-looking, and fits easily on a bathroom counter without taking up much space. What stands out the most is how effective the cleaning is. I use it mainly for retainers and after each cycle they come out noticeably cleaner and fresher. It removes buildup that regular brushing doesn’t always get rid of, which makes it feel much more hygienic. The cleaner is also very easy to use. You simply add water, place the dental item inside, and press a button. The cleaning cycle is quick, and the device operates quietly, which is a nice bonus. I also appreciate the build quality, it feels sturdy and well made. Another positive is that it’s gentle on dental appliances. I feel confident using it daily without worrying about damage, which is important for expensive retainers. Overall, the ZIMA Dental Pod Pro is a great investment for anyone who uses dental appliances and wants a deeper clean with minimal effort. It’s convenient, effective, and well-designed, making it a product I’d definitely recommend.

Name: Alyassa T Hatter
Review Content: After reading positive reviews, I decided to try the Zima Dental Pod Pro. The device is compact, well-designed, and easy to use, making it a seamless addition to my daily routine. I followed the instructions and used the recommended cleaning tablets with the device for a two week period. I noticed a dramatic improvement over this period. The years of accumulated scaling had loosened and, with just a little gentle prodding, came off completely. The results were instant and impressive—my dental appliance looked almost brand new, free from the unsightly buildup that had bothered me for so long. I highly recommend this product.

Review Content: Before and after of my invisilign retainers. I’m really impressed! I fill it with water and plop an Extra Strength Retainer Cleaner in and then press the button twice for a ten minute deep clean. Works wonders.

Name: Bones
Review Content: I was hoping the container would have been a little larger, but it seams like a good.

Name: Giraffe Sandals
Review Content: As someone who wears a mouth splint at night to stop from grinding my teeth, I thought I would try out this ultrasonic cleaning device. I have never tried one before so I was excited! The machine is compact, yet a good size for retainers, mouth splints, jewelry, or toothbrush heads. I also like to use it for the little brush heads from my sulcus brushes. There are 2 different time settings - 300 seconds (which is 5 minutes, not 3 minutes), or 600 seconds (which is 10 minutes, not 6). There are also options of UV and/or ultrasonic cleaning. It comes with some plastic tweezers to grab your items out of the cleaner, which is nice. It is easy to use, with only one button that you can cycle through to see the options. It's not overly complicated, the size is nice, the cord is plenty long enough, and it's not a bad looking device that has some smart features. The price is also very attractive. Overall, I recommend this unit and fully intend to go through all my old jewelry and get it all cleaned up!

Name: Amanda KT
Review Content: If my night guard could talk, it would probably beg me never to go back to the days before this ultrasonic cleaner entered our lives. I recently had to start wearing a night guard (thank you, chipped tooth, for your surprise cameo in my dental x‑rays), and while I love what it does for my jaw, cleaning it quickly became the least glamorous part of my morning routine. The dentist told me to brush it with tooth paste, or use an ultrasonic cleaner, but who has one of those (her words). I am so glad I ordered this cleaner because now I can save my previous morning time doing other things. First of all, using it makes me feel like a dentist—or at least a dentist’s enthusiastic intern. You just toss the night guard in, add some water, push a button, and suddenly it’s like your appliance is spending the morning at a five‑star resort. Meanwhile, you get to sip coffee and pretend you’ve got your life together. The machine uses these tiny vibrations that I can’t see but fully believe in because my night guard comes out looking like it just got back from a rejuvenating wellness retreat. All the little crevices I could never reach with a brush? Sparkling. That faint funky smell you don’t want to admit is definitely there? Gone. My night guard practically glows with self‑confidence. I even started tossing other things in there—earrings, retainers, that one ring I forgot I owned. Everything emerges cleaner than my conscience after flossing for the first time in three months. Best of all, it’s stupidly easy. No complicated buttons, no weird noises, no rituals involving chanting under the full moon. Just hit start, let the magic bubbles do their thing, and enjoy knowing your dentist would be proud. If you’ve got a night guard, retainer, aligner, or simply enjoy watching tiny bubbles do big work, this ultrasonic cleaner is absolutely worth it. My night guard and I have never been happier—and we’re sleeping better too. Read more

Name: Just some Canadian
Review Content: Overall, I feel that this is a robust, full featured UV/ultrasonic cleaner. It’s ideal for anyone with Invisalign’s, retainers, or sports mouth guards and can also bring some nice sparkle to jewelry. I grabbed this cleaning unit for my Invisalign retainers. I started using a cleaning device like this during treatment, when I was wearing the liners all day and having to take them out every time to eat—it was wonderful to not have to put a smelly or unsanized liner back in your mouth after doing that to to be able to clean them with just a button. Now that I just have to wear them at night, I still use a cleaner like this because it dos such a good job and is so easy. They do wear out eventually and I’m always interested in the latest tech (maybe someone’s made an even better version), so I grabbed this unit to try out. After a week of use, the result is that I’m leaving my old cleaner in the closet as a backup. This one does as good a job on its 5 minute cycle as my old was doing on its longer cycle. This one also runs a little quieter (although FYI there is a quiet buzzing sound to it when the sonic feature is on). This one shows you how many seconds are remaining on the cleaning cycle, which my other one doesn’t have. So between those things, this product feels like an upgrade. I will sometimes run it with just water and sometimes add a special cleaning liquid meant for these UV cleaners with retainers. For anyone who has invisaligns, retainers, or sports mouth guards, this type of cleaning device is a huge life upgrade. It helps with discoloration, gets rid of smell, and ensures it’s not full of bacteria. If you or a loved one has any of those, I highly recommend one. For those who don’t have retainers like I do, this device can also be used for jewelry or toothbrushes. There are slots at the top for the head of the toothbrush to get stuck though. I have a separate wall mounted uv toothbrush cleaner that I prefer for that, but I like that the toothbrush slots are there because they show a tint from the UV light when it’s on, so that you can visually confirm that it’s activated. This device also does have two different durations of settings and the ability to use both UV and ultrasonic with them or one or the other. There are more options for that than with my previous cleaner like this too. So far, it’s performing well and seems quite durable. It’s a tiny bit heavier than my previous one and I like the hard press button (rather than a touch screen style one). It is a plug in model, but I don’t mind that as I’ve had poor luck with rechargeable ones stopping working before their time. I also did note that the plug has a proper UL certification on it and it did not feel warm after use. Read more

Name: Jimmy J.
Review Content: I've been using this ultrasonic cleaner for about a month now for some heavy duty cleanup to go through about 1600 lego pieces that have been through some rough abuse. Most of the lego pieces are covered in scum since they've been reused all the time without much maintenance for over 10 years. With warm water and some dish soap I've been slowly but steadily getting through them about 6 to 12 pieces at a time in this cleaner. The cavitation works, dirt and residue lift off surprisingly well in 5 minute cycles. Lego pieces comes out like new. Good thing the cleaner comes with a stainless steel basket that keeps the pieces from scratching the tank bottom and makes retrieval easy. The ultrasonic is really quiet, not disruptive when I've sometimes been running it all day back to back. This ultrasonic cleaner is very valuable to me because if I toss my lego pieces into the laundry machine they are going to destroy it. In the future I will also find new use for this cleaner, so I'm going to give it 5 stars for the value it provides. Definitely recommend.

Name: Annavi
Review Content: When I ordered this I figured it would be a basic gadget that might save me a few minutes on cleaning my retainer each night, nothing more than that. I wasn't looking for a professional grade device, just something small and practical that could do a better job than my toothbrush and some warm water. After nearly a week of daily use I can say it's exceeded those initial expectations in ways I didn't anticipate. The cleaning results on my retainer have been consistently better than what I achieved by hand, the pulsed ultrasonic mode in particular leaves everything looking and smelling noticeably fresher without any risk of the scratching that always concerned me when I used a brush. The size of the unit is another area where it's performed beyond what I hoped for, because at about 11 cm tall with a 200 ml tank it takes up almost no counter space and I've been keeping it on my bathroom shelf without any issue. The noise level is low enough that I've run cleaning cycles late in the evening without disturbing anyone in the house, and the single button operation means there's nothing complicated about switching between the four available modes. The only thing I'd flag is the fixed steel cup making cleanup a bit awkward, but that's a minor point. Every expectation met and then some. Read more

Name: Tabasco15
Review Content: This had super fast shipping so I chose this ultrasonic cleaner. It came in a nice box and was well packaged. It also came with a cleaning cloth. Tweezers, and some cleaning tablets. Set up is easy just plug it in. There is a line that shows you how much water to use. Add the tablets and push the button on the front of the cleaner. It will work for 5 minutes and will shut off automatically. I tested it out with my rings first. The tablets are not very good but you can easily find others. I have seen that they make a cleaning solution so I will try that out next. It works great! My rings came out shiny and clean. Perfect! It looks great on the counter too. As a bonus I got it on sale as well and I couldn’t be happier. I highly recommend this one.

Name: Barkeller
Review Content: I recently started using this product for aligners and it cleans very well in a short amount of time. Am very pleased with results.

Name: Travelinggc
Review Content: I've only used it once but I was pleased with the results

Review Content: Love it so easy to use

Review Content: This solution in my ultrasonic cleaner cleans my Invisalign trays beautifully.

Name: Raidel Gonzalez Cabrera
Review Content: I’ve been using this ultrasonic cleaner for my retainers and I’m honestly impressed. It’s very easy to use just add water, press the button, and it does its job in a few minutes. After each cycle, my retainers come out noticeably cleaner, with no odor and no residue. The size is perfect, not too bulky, and it looks modern on the counter. I also like that it can be used for other items like jewelry, which is a nice bonus. The ultrasonic vibration seems strong enough to clean deeply without damaging anything.

Name: Tricky
Review Content: Currently a five star review because when combined with the Weiss Naturals Retainer/Invisalign Cleaner tablets pictured in this review, our INVISALIGN retainers are noticeably cleaner and staining has been reduced. Easy to simultaneously clean two sets of INVISALIGN. Simple directions and operation. Unplug + fill + use + unplug + drain = safest method of operation. Nice enough color and design so that it can be left on bathroom counter if you have an outlet nearby. Metal tub so don’t plan on cleaning your jewelry in this device so versatility is limited. Photo accurately depicts the Ultrasonic Cleaner and the included components. Appears to be of good quality and I’ll repost if there are any issues with durability. Hope that this review is useful/helpful. ☮️

Name: Charis
Review Content: This ultrasonic retainer cleaner works great! It’s compact, easy to use, and does an excellent job cleaning my retainers. I only needed to lightly brush them before using this cleaner for 10 minutes, and my retainers looked like new afterward. It’s very quiet, and the compact size makes it perfect for travel. I also like that it can be used for jewelry and toothbrush heads too. Definitely worth the purchase!

Name: Jordenia
Review Content: Title: Easy and Convenient Cleaner for Retainers I’ve been using this ultrasonic cleaner for my retainers and it works really well. It’s super easy to use and helps remove buildup and odors much better than just rinsing with water. I also like that it’s compact and doesn’t take up much space in the bathroom. My retainers feel fresher and cleaner after every use. Definitely worth it!

Name: MommaBear
Review Content: This ultrasonic dental appliance cleaner is just what I was looking for. It is small, compact, easy to use and easy to clean. I use this to clean Invisalign retainers. The water and vibration alone will not clean your retainers as well, you definitely need to use Efferdent or another retainer cleaning tablet. I have found that it is best to place the tablet in the water and let it dissolve before adding the dental appliances. I use the first mode which cleans for three minutes and uses UV light to kill any germs. I’m not sure that any dental cleaning machine will clean really well without a cleaning tablet, so that was not a big deal to me to use one. If I could give this 4 1/2 stars, I would, due to the fact that there is no battery option. The cord can become a little annoying if you only have a small space on your sink top or do not have easy access to an outlet. I’ve only been using this cleaner for a few weeks, but so far I feel it is a good quality.

Name: Elaine Pandolfe
Review Content: Previously, I used bleaching tablets to clean my braces, but after cleaning, they still felt slippery and there were still blind spots. The upgraded ultrasonic cleaner actually performs real physical vibration to remove dirt. After cleaning, it feels "gritty" which indicates true cleanliness. There's no need to buy various cleaning tablets anymore. Just using plain water can do the job. In the long run, it saves money and truly solves the hygiene problem.

Name: Courtney
Review Content: So far I have cleaned retainers and some jewelry. The jewelry is the reason for 4 stars. I had cleaned my wedding ring and some cheap earrings. The ring looks great but the earrings still had some debris on the inner side of them. Probably would have come out in another wash it 2. They size is great for counter top, seems durable. I am terrified that it'll get wet and stop working but hopefully that won't be an issue. I do love that I can clean multiple different items with this all for the same price.

Love the cleaner and very happy I can do this on my own a few times a week instead of depending on it getting clean with my dentist visits! Highly recommend the machine!

Name: Alis
Review Content: I have been using this 48 kHz ultrasonic cleaning machine for my retainers and I was really surprised how well it works. From the first use you can tell the difference: it leaves the retainers much cleaner and without residue, even in areas where normal brushing is not enough. The process is very simple. I just add water, put the retainer and in a few minutes it's ready. It does not make excessive noise and the size is perfect for use at home without taking up much space. I also like that it works for various items, such as dentures, aligners, mouthguards and even brush heads, which makes it quite versatile. As for the cleaning, I feel that it is deep but without damaging the materials, which is very important. In addition, it helps maintain better oral hygiene without having to use strong chemicals.

Name: Benjamin Stephanus
Review Content: This cleaner is incredibly easy to use—just add water, put your retainer or aligner in, and press the button. The 48kHz ultrasonic cleaning works wonders, gently removing all dirt and buildup without any scrubbing at all.I really appreciate the digital timer, which lets me set it and move on with my day. It’s compact, quiet, and the 200ml tank is just the right size for retainers and even toothbrush heads. I’ve also used it for my jewelry, and the results were great.I highly recommend this to anyone with dental appliances—it saves so much time and cleans far better than hand washing ever could.

Name: Stephen Shumaker
Review Content: It removes all the stubborn plaque and buildup that manual brushing couldn't reach. It feels much more hygienic than just soaking them in a cup of water. The compact design fits perfectly on my crowded bathroom counter without taking up much space.

Name: Andre A Melikyan
Review Content: The UV light shuts off automatically when the lid is opened, which is a great safety feature. This is a massive upgrade over manually scrubbing with a toothbrush and soap. No more weird odors or cloudy film on my clear aligners.

Review Content: It removes all the stubborn plaque and buildup that manual brushing couldn't reach. The water gets noticeably cloudy, which proves how much gunk it actually removes. The compact design fits perfectly on my crowded bathroom counter without taking up much space.
